item.title: How China Is Pushing Back Against the West’s De-Risking Strategy
item.description: Much of the world relies on China for an increasing number of products. Beijing wants to keep it that way.

item.title: Modest ECB Rate Hike Would Limit Economic Pain, Stournaras Says
item.description: A small European Central Bank interest-rate increase could temper inflation without causing economic damage, Governing Council member Yannis Stournaras told Liberal.gr.

item.title: Elon Musk Says He Isn’t Selling His SpaceX Shares as IPO Looms
item.description: Elon Musk said he is not selling any SpaceX shares, with the company reported to be preparing to file publicly for its long-awaited IPO as soon as next week.

item.title: Charting the Global Economy: Inflation Mounts as War Drags On
item.description: The fallout from the war in Iran continued to ripple across the global economy, complicating the outlook and fueling inflation angst.

item.title: Stripe's John Collison on How Agentic Commerce Will Reshape the Internet
item.description: Adapting to a world where AI agents are doing the shopping.

item.title: Odd Lots: Stripe’s John Collison on Agentic Commerce (Podcast)
item.description: The internet is made for shopping. For years, the main inputs for e-commerce transactions involved targeted ads, algorithmic recommendations, SEO, and lots of mindless scrolling. But agentic commerce might represent a sea change for e-commerce: With the rise of AI agents doing shopping on behalf of consumers, how are retailers going to adapt? John Collison, co-founder of the financial services and payment processing company Stripe, has first-hand experience with all the ways e-commerce has chang

item.title: China Needs to Modernize Older Sectors, Not Scrap Them, Minister Says
item.description: China should upgrade “outdated” industries and not scrap them because manufacturing remains the backbone of the economy, according to the Minister of Industry and Information Technology Li Lecheng.

item.title: Without Migrants, Spain Says Bars Would Shut and Services Suffer
item.description: The opposition parties seeking to shut down Madrid’s pro-migrant policies are jeopardizing key pillars of Spanish life, the Socialist-led government says.

item.title: China Data Centers Tap Spot Power Trading First Time: Report
item.description: China’s large-scale data centers have joined electricity spot trading as virtual power plants for the first time, marking a shift in how computing demand interacts with the grid, a report in an official media said.

item.title: Europe Lacks Everything Needed to Make Its Stock Market a Winner
item.description: The equity story that powered European stocks has unraveled as investors seek shelter from a global energy shock and buy into the artificial intelligence frenzy.

item.title: Long Island Rail Workers Strike in First Walkout Since 1994
item.description: The Long Island Rail Road, the nation’s busiest commuter line, will suspend service for the first time in more than 30 years after labor groups and transit officials failed to reach a deal on wage increases by a Friday night deadline.

item.title: Rare Ebola Strain With No Approved Vaccine Kills Dozens in Congo
item.description: A rare strain of Ebola with no approved vaccine or treatment may have circulated undetected for weeks in conflict-hit northeastern Democratic Republic of Congo before killing dozens of people.

item.title: Trump Says US Forces, Nigeria Armed Forces Killed ISIS Leader
item.description: US and Nigerian forces killed an ISIS leader who was hiding in Africa, President Donald Trump said, adding that his death has “greatly diminished” the group’s global operations.

item.title: VinFast Seeks to Shed Most Debt With Vietnam Factory Spinoffs
item.description: VinFast Auto Ltd. said its planned sale of two Vietnamese factories will enable the electric vehicle maker to shed about 182 trillion dong ($6.9 billion) in debt and obligations while potentially speed its path toward profitability.

item.title: SpaceX Shareholders Approve 5-for-1 Split of Common Stock
item.description: A majority of SpaceX shareholders approved a 5-for-1 stock split recommended by the closely held company’s board, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: NextEra Energy Is Said to Be in Talks to Acquire Dominion
item.description: NextEra Energy Inc. is in discussions to acquire utility rival Dominion Energy Inc. in a mostly stock deal aimed at helping address the growing demand for power from data centers, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: NextEra Energy Is in Talks to Combine With Dominion Energy: FT
item.description: NextEra Energy Inc. is in discussions to combine with utility rival Dominion Energy Inc. in a mostly stock deal, the Financial Times reported.

item.title: Adnoc Keeps Loading LNG Onto Tankers Gone Dark in Persian Gulf
item.description: Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. is continuing to load liquefied natural gas onto tankers masking their location in the Persian Gulf, as the energy producer pushes to get more fuel through the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Korea Bond Yields May Extend Gains on Chips Boom, Analysts Say
item.description: South Korea’s bond are set to extend this year’s rout as a semiconductor boom supercharges the nation’s economic growth and adds to inflationary pressures, analysts say.

item.title: Trump Chats With Japan’s Takaichi on Alliance After Xi Meeting
item.description: Donald Trump spoke by phone with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi and they affirmed the strength of the two countries’ alliance after the US President’s visit to Beijing, according to the Japanese government.

item.title: Snap, YouTube Settle School-Social Media Suit Ahead of Trial
item.description: Google’s YouTube and Snap Inc. reached agreements to settle the first lawsuit headed to trial over claims that addiction to top social media platforms has disrupted learning and pushed public schools to spend massive sums fighting a mental health crisis, according to court filings.

item.title: Berkshire Sold $8 Billion of Chevron Shares as Prices Soared
item.description: Berkshire Hathaway Inc. sold about $8 billion worth of Chevron Corp. shares in the first quarter as the oil giant’s stock reached a record high.

item.title: Arm Holdings to Face US Antitrust Probe Over Chip Tech
item.description: Arm Holdings Plc is facing an antitrust investigation by the US Federal Trade Commission over the UK company’s licensing of its semiconductor technology, part of ongoing global scrutiny of the business, people familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Forbright Files for IPO Pitching Middle Market, Digital Banking
item.description: Forbright Inc., a financial services platform that focuses on middle-market lending and digital consumer banking, filed for an initial public offering showing growing deposits.

item.title: Erock, a Power Systems Maker for Data Centers, Files for US IPO
item.description: Erock Inc., a modular power systems maker serving data centers and other sectors, filed for a US initial public offering, disclosing increasing revenue and widening losses.

item.title: From Trade to Taiwan, What the Trump-Xi Summit Did (and Didn’t) Accomplish
item.description: On today’s Big Take podcast: President Trump’s summit with Xi Jinping offered good optics, but gave little indication of progress on major sticking points.

item.title: Fed Names Powell Chair Pro Tempore Until Warsh Sworn In
item.description: The Federal Reserve board on Friday named Jerome Powell chair pro tempore until his successor Kevin Warsh is officially sworn in, the central bank said in a statement.

item.title: Berkshire Boosts Alphabet, Exits Amazon in CEO’s First Quarter
item.description: Greg Abel spent his first months as chief executive officer of Berkshire Hathaway Inc. boosting the conglomerate’s holding in Alphabet Inc. while exiting its bet on Amazon.com.

item.title: The Global Bond Rout Catches Up With Wall Street’s Risk Rally
item.description: Wall Street’s hard-charging risk rally just ran into the bond market.

item.title: Argentina’s YPF Seeks Incentives for $25 Billion Oil Project
item.description: State-run Argentine energy giant YPF SA announced a $25 billion project to accelerate oil production in the country, marking the largest investment since President Javier Milei took office.
